file_insights.key_content: Progress journal documenting verification completion of install_hooks_linux implementation. Documents prior session (make check passed with 446 tests, zero clippy warnings) and current session verification of 8 requirements (4 install, 4 uninstall).

Structured 2-phase plan: Phase 1 (Verification: subtasks 1-1 complete, 1-2 complete) and Phase 2 (Documentation: subtask 2-1 pending). Both phases marked sequential (parallel_safe: false). Includes verification strategy with manual review instructions for factory.rs lines 359-415 (install) and 639-674 (uninstall).

patterns_discovered.description: Subtask involved no code modifications - only reviewing existing implementation (factory.rs) against requirements checklist. All 8 requirements pre-existing and verified against spec.

Linux install-hooks feature uses userspace systemd services: generates .service and .path files, writes to ~/.config/systemd/user/, runs systemctl --user daemon-reload/enable/start. Uninstall mirrors this with stop/disable/remove/daemon-reload.
patterns_discovered.technical_detail: Two systemd units managed: bop.service and bop.path

patterns_discovered.description: Uninstall function (lines 639-674) uses `let _` pattern to ignore errors on systemctl stop/disable and file removal operations. Ensures cleanup completes even if intermediate steps fail.

Verification workflow captured in JSON implementation_plan.json with explicit phases, subtasks, and verification instructions. Manual verification type specified with exact line ranges to review (359-415, 639-674).
Phase 1 (Verification: 2 subtasks) must complete before Phase 2 (Documentation: 1 subtask) can begin. Explicitly marked as non-parallel.

gotchas_discovered.description: Subtask-1-2 verification uses manual code review against requirements checklist rather than automated tests. Verification instructions embedded in implementation_plan.json specify exact factory.rs line ranges to audit.
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Could create dedicated unit tests for install_hooks_linux function, but current approach acceptable for verification-only task

gotchas_discovered.description: Implementation uses `systemctl --user` and ~/.config/systemd/user/ directory (user scope), not system-wide systemd. Important distinction for deployment and permissions.
gotchas_discovered.technical_note: Appropriate for user-facing daemon; requires no sudo

gotchas_discovered.description: Uninstall function (lines 648-674) uses best-effort pattern - silently ignores errors on systemctl operations and file removal. Could mask actual failure conditions.
gotchas_discovered.design_rationale: Ensures cleanup completes even if some operations fail (e.g., service already stopped)

gotchas_discovered.description: Verification checklist has 8 separate requirements: 4 for install path (write .service/.path, daemon-reload, enable both, start both) and 4 for uninstall (stop both, disable both, remove both files, daemon-reload). Easy to miss any one.
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Checklist explicitly documented in build-progress.txt subtask-1-2 section

approach_outcome.test_results: Prior session (subtask-1-1): make check passed - 446 tests + zero clippy warnings + format check passed
approach_outcome.outcome_summary: Successfully verified that install_hooks_linux implementation meets all 8 requirements (4 install + 4 uninstall). No code changes needed. Documentation files created to track verification. Ready to proceed to Phase 2 (create output/result.md).

recommendations.recommendation: Execute subtask-2-1: Create output/result.md documenting verification results (make check status, clippy warnings count, install_hooks_linux completeness summary, uninstall verification)

Create dedicated unit tests for cmd_factory_install_linux() and cmd_factory_uninstall_linux() functions to automate verification of the 8 requirements. Current manual verification is sound but could be codified.
Extract systemd service and path file generation (generate_systemd_service, generate_systemd_path) into separate factory module for reusability and testability
Document the best-effort error handling pattern used in uninstall (let _ pattern) with comment explaining why errors are intentionally ignored during cleanup
Continue using auto-claude structured verification pattern with JSON implementation_plan.json - enables clear phase dependencies and subtask tracking across multiple sessions
